title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Quantum Accelerator, New England's only dual-launch straddle coaster, opens at Six Flags New England on April 11.
The coaster features two launches, taking riders to speeds of 30 mph and 45 mph, and includes 11 airtime moments.
Quantum Accelerator is the twelfth roller coaster at Six Flags New England, with the park spanning 200 acres.

title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston will require AI training for all public high school graduates starting September, Mayor Michelle Wu announced.
The program will be funded by a $1 million grant from Kayak co-founder Paul English, supporting teacher training.
AI curriculum developed by UMass Boston focuses on ethical, critical engagement, preparing students for college and careers.

title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a court filing after confusion over the paternity of Sandra Birchmore's unborn child.
The original filing mistakenly implied Farwell's twin brother fathered Birchmore's child; subsequent DNA tests proved otherwise.
Matthew Farwell, accused of killing Birchmore in 2021, has pleaded not guilty and seeks release on $50,000 bail.

title: A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lily Penzone, a 21-year-old from Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her best friend's late father.
Penzone is part of Brigham and Women’s Stepping Strong team, supporting trauma research and recovery through her run.
The run pays tribute to Abby's father, a former Boston Marathon runner, and his legacy of generosity and support.

title: Boston College senior running Boston Marathon to support first responders
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Merideth Greene, a Boston College senior, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor and support firefighters.
She is raising awareness for the Last Call Foundation, focusing on firefighter health risks and preventive care.
Growing up in Watertown, Greene was inspired by first responders during and after the 2013 Marathon bombing.

title: Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Peach Farm in Chinatown is closing temporarily as the current owners retire and new ownership takes over.
Owner Tom Leung is retiring after 31 years; new ownership is likely to maintain the restaurant's name and concept.
The transfer of Peach Farm's licenses was approved, with Washan Ching listed as the new manager and part-owner.

title: Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts U.S. Attorney Leah Foley announced a new federal task force to investigate benefit and voter fraud statewide.
The task force, led by Philip Mallard and Mark Grady, will target SNAP, MassHealth, and election-related fraud.
The ACLU of Massachusetts criticized the inclusion of voter fraud, describing it as a myth and not a substantial issue.

title: United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces Relax Row, transforming three economy seats into a couch, available starting 2027.
Relax Row features a mattress-like space with adjustable leg rests, suitable for families and long-haul travelers.
United plans to equip more than 200 Boeing 787 and 777 aircraft with Relax Row by 2030.

title: Ad Club’s Women’s Leadership Forum stays alive amid DEI pushback
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Ad Club's Women's Leadership Forum was held at Gillette Stadium, featuring speakers, panel discussions, and a small business marketplace.
The forum faced challenges due to pushback against DEI programs but continued to support women's networking and professional development.
This year's theme 'Unbound' encouraged attendees to define success individually, highlighting resilience amid changing corporate climates.

title: Juvenile injured in Allston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A juvenile was injured in a shooting on Thursday evening near 222 Brighton Ave., Allston.
The victim suffered non-life-threatening injuries and was treated at a local hospital, police reported.
Boston police have not made any arrests and the shooter fled the scene after the incident.
